Title: Generic .tres importer
Summary: How Bleepforge reads .tres files for any manifest-declared domain â€” handler dispatch table, override registry for FoB's bespoke importers, sub-resource recursion with _subId, AtlasTexture preservation contract. Built in v0.2.8; production dispatch lands in v0.2.9 alongside the FoB port.
Body:
## The shape

Mirror of v0.2.7's writer. Every `.tres â†’ JSON` conversion for a manifest-discovered domain flows through `readFromManifest(parsed, entry, ctx)` in [server/src/internal/import/generic/orchestrator.ts](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/server/src/internal/import/generic/orchestrator.ts).

A per-file dispatcher (`readTres` in `reader.ts`) consults the **override registry** first. FoB's seven hand-rolled importers register as overrides at module init â€” they keep running unchanged for the seven hardcoded domains (item / quest / karma / npc / faction / dialog / balloon). When the override matches, the bespoke importer handles the read and the generic path is skipped.

When no override matches, the dispatcher falls through to the generic path â€” a manifest-driven reader composed of 10 handler files covering all 12 manifest field types (`string` + `multiline` + `flag` share `string.ts`; `int` + `float` share `numeric.ts`).

## The 10 handlers

Each handler reads one manifest field type. Uniform signature so the orchestrator dispatches via a flat lookup table:

```ts
type FieldReader = (
  tresValue: TresValue | undefined,
  fieldDef: FieldDef,
  propName: string,
  ctx: ReaderContext,
) => unknown;
```

`tresValue` is `undefined` when the property isn't in the source `.tres` â€” the handler returns `FieldDef.default ?? type-natural-zero` (empty string / 0 / false / first enum value). Symmetric with the writer's "omit when matching default" behavior so default-aware emit round-trips cleanly.

**Scalar handlers** (in `handlers/`):

- `string` / `multiline` / `flag` (all in `string.ts`) â€” same Godot quoted-string form. TresValue kinds `string` + `raw` (parser fallback) both pass through verbatim.
- `int` / `float` (both in `numeric.ts`) â€” number form. `intHandler` truncates via `Math.trunc` to enforce integer shape in JSON.
- `bool` â€” boolean form.
- `enum` â€” Godot stores enum index; reader maps to `FieldDef.values[idx]`. Index 0 is Godot's omission default so an absent property returns `values[0]`. Out-of-range index produces a warning + values[0] fallback.

**Reference handlers**:

- `ref` (`ref.ts`) â€” cross-domain Resource reference. Calls `ctx.resolveRefByExtResource(ext, targetDomain)` â€” ProjectIndex-backed in the wired path. **Dangling refs** (target not in index) stay as the verbatim `res://` string with a warning; matches the writer's tolerance for dangling refs in JSON.
- `texture` (`texture.ts`) â€” Texture2D. Two non-obvious cases: **ExtResource â†’ absolute fs path** (via `ctx.resPathToAbs` â€” Bleepforge's image pipeline serves flat PNG bytes, JSON holds absolute paths) and **AtlasTexture preservation contract** (SubResource pointing at an AtlasTexture â†’ JSON gets `""`; the writer's existing `reconcileTextureField` preserves the AtlasTexture on save when JSON is empty). Phase 5's round-trip harness validates that contract from both ends.
- `scene` (`scene.ts`) â€” PackedScene. ExtResource â†’ res:// path verbatim. Scenes are always relative to the Godot project root (Godot loads them through its own resource system); res:// form is portable across machines.

**Composite handlers** (recurse through the orchestrator):

- `array` (`array.ts`) â€” two flavors discriminated by manifest fields:
  - `array.of: "SubResourceName"` â†’ sub-resource arrays. Each entry is a `SubResource(id)` ref; the handler looks up the sub_resource section in `parsed.subResources`, recurses into `orchestrator.readFlatFields` against the sub-resource's declared fields. **`_subId` populates** from the section id so the writer's stable-identity matching survives reorder / edit / remove on round-trip.
  - `array.itemRef: { to: "domain" }` â†’ ref arrays (NPC `CasualRemarks` shape). Each entry resolves through `resolveRefByExtResource`; dangling entries stay as res:// strings.
  - Absent property OR empty array â†’ JSON `[]`. Wrong TresValue kind â†’ warning + `[]`.
- `subresource` (`subresource.ts`) â€” single inline sub_resource (NPC `LootTable` shape). The canonical FoB instance: LootTable wraps `Entries: LootEntry[]`, so the subresource handler recurses into the array handler, which recurses back into the subresource handler per entry. Unlike array entries, single subresources are positionally identified â€” they ARE the host field â€” so `_subId` is NOT populated at the top level.
  - Absent â†’ `null`. SubResource not declared in manifest â†’ warning + `null`.

## Resolver injection

The handlers don't read from global singletons. `ReaderContext` carries the three resolvers the caller populates:

```ts
interface ReaderContext {
  godotRoot: string;
  filePath: string;
  parsed: ParsedTres;
  warnings: string[];
  resolveRefByExtResource: (ext, targetDomain) => string | null;
  resPathToAbs: (resPath) => string;
  subResources: Map<string, SubResource>;
}
```

Production wiring: resolvers are backed by `projectIndex` + the canonical `resPathToAbs` helper. Boot reconcile + watcher reimport both build the context the same way (see `lib/reconcile/manifestReconcile.ts` + `lib/manifest/reimport.ts`).

## Single-file dispatch

The watcher's per-file path uses `reimportOneManifest(absPath, godotRoot)` in [server/src/lib/manifest/reimport.ts](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/server/src/lib/manifest/reimport.ts). Shape mirrors FoB's `reimportOne`: parse the .tres, run `readFromManifest`, write the JSON via `storage.writeEntity`, publish the sync event. `detectDomain` in `reimportOne.ts` returns a `{ kind: "fob" | "manifest" }` discriminator so the watcher dispatches to either the FoB switch or the manifest branch without duplicate dispatch logic.

## Validation

Four smoke suites in `server/src/internal/import/generic/`:

- `scalarsSmoke.ts` â€” 16 cases covering string / multiline / flag / int / float / bool / enum + `showWhen` gating + default-aware reads.
- `refsSmoke.ts` â€” 8 cases for ref / texture / scene, including dangling refs + AtlasTexture preservation.
- `arraysSmoke.ts` â€” 7 cases for sub-resource arrays (with `_subId` populated) + ref arrays + missing decl handling.
- `subresourceSmoke.ts` â€” 4 cases for the NPC LootTable shape including nested array recursion.

Plus two end-to-end validation gates:

- `import/genericKarmaHarness.ts` â€” runs the generic reader against every `.tres` in the FoB karma corpus (6 files) and compares to `mapKarma`'s output via canonical key-sorted JSON. 6/6 matched. Proves the reader half is JSON-equivalent to FoB's hand-rolled importer.
- `tres/genericRoundTripHarness.ts` â€” the contract gate. For 5 domains (karma / faction / balloon / dialog / quest), runs `parse â†’ readFromManifest â†’ writeFromManifest â†’ emit` and compares byte-identical to the original. **42/59 clean**, 14 limit-multiline (parser/writer helper-layer fix tracked separately), 3 differs in quest files with stale FoB data violating the manifest's `showWhen` contract.

Run via `pnpm --filter @bleepforge/server smoke:import-{scalars,refs,arrays,subresource}` + `pnpm --filter @bleepforge/server harness:import-karma` + `pnpm --filter @bleepforge/server harness:round-trip`.

## What's deferred

- **Production dispatch through the generic path.** Today every FoB read still flows through its override (registered for safety while the architecture lands); the generic path is exercised by smoke + harness only. v0.2.9 wires production saves through `writeTresGeneric` once the edit UI lands.
- **`discriminatedFamily` kind.** The orchestrator refuses these with a clear reason; boot reconcile records them as `skipped`. Will land alongside v0.2.9's edit UI (ItemData / QuestItemData / Equipment shape).
- **Multi-line string round-trip.** Lossy parser's continuation-line `.trim()` + space-join collapses physical newlines inside quoted strings to spaces (`tresParser.ts:131`); writer's `serializeString` emits `\n` as escape. Two-spot helper fix needed for byte-identical multi-line round-trip; tracked separately.

- [.tres is truth, JSON is cache](/help/under-the-hood/tres-is-truth) â€” the data model.
